LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLARIFY THAT PLAINTIFF IS ALLEGING A VICARIOUS LIABILITY CAUSE OF ACTION PER GOV'T CODE 815.2 AND/OR A DIRECT CLAIM PER GOV'T CODE 815 AND CIVIL CODE 2100. A CLAIM AGAINST A PUBLIC ENTITY MUST BE PLEADED WITH PARTICULARITY, INCLUDING THE EXISTENCE AND SOURCE OF A STATUTORY DUTY. (SEARCY V. HEMET UNIFIED SCHOOL DIST. (1986) 177 CAL.APP.3D 792, 802.)(SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS)ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JUL-17-2019 CONTINUED TO AUG-28-2019 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff Complaint
Off Calendar May-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Matter on calendar for Monday, April 15, 2019, Line

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff Complaint.

The motion to strike is off calendar as moot.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:
Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 8, 2019, Line 13,

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the third cause of action in plaintiff's complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to amend to clarify that Plaintiff is alleging a vicarious liability cause of action per Gov't Code 815.2 and/or a direct claim per Gov't Code 815 and Civil Code 2100. A claim against a public entity must be pleaded with particularity, including the existence and source of a statutory duty. (Searcy v. Hemet Unified School Dist. (1986) 177 Cal.App.3d 792, 802.)

Plaintiff can allege a vicarious liability claim based on conduct by City employees. Plaintiff alleges a sufficient "special relationship" to support the claim. (See C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 868-71, 877; Lopez v. Co. Cal. Rapid Transit (1985) 40 Cal.3d 780, 789 ["We believe these characteristics of public transportation, along with the duty of utmost care and diligence imposed by Civil Code section 2100, provide a more than ample basis for finding a special relationship between common carriers and their passengers."].)

Plaintiff can allege a direct claim against the City pursuant to Lopez v. Co. Cal. Rapid Transit (1985) 40 Cal.3d 780, 785, fn. 2.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
